title: Cross-chain borrow lacks source chain verification allowing stale collateral use
reason: Duplicate of existing F-002 (same stale collateral snapshot/TOCTOU root cause and exploit path).

title: LEND reward claim allows permissionless claim for any address array
reason: Not reportable as fund-theft: claims are transferred to the specified holders, not caller; this is generally permissionless-claim behavior and does not add material harm beyond existing F-014.

title: Cross-chain repay uses wrong chain identifier for same-chain storage
reason: Substantially overlaps existing F-003/F-017 accounting flaws; no distinct new root cause established.

title: Cross-chain liquidation failure sends tokens without balance verification
reason: Already captured by F-019 (refund without escrow / failure-path payout mismatch).

title: Cross-chain repay validates only srcEid without verifying borrow position exists on that chain
reason: Duplicate of existing F-012 (repay lookup keyed only by `srcEid`, ambiguous position selection).

title: No access control on authorizedContracts - any authorized caller can modify any user state
reason: Trust-model observation, not a protocol bug by itself; privileged authorized contracts are expected to mutate user state.

title: Oracle price of zero creates fail-open liquidity check enabling unauthorized borrows
reason: Duplicate of existing F-015 (zero-price fail-open in liquidity checks).

title: Cross-chain liquidation uses seize amount as repayment without verifying actual debt exists
reason: Covered by existing F-013/F-018 liquidation variable misuse; reported scenario depends on the same underlying flaw.
